CURMUDGEON CORNER
Attention is all you need? When responsiveness short‑circuits 
responsibility
Emre Sezgin1,2
Received: 10 September 2025 / Accepted: 18 October 2025 
© The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer-Verlag London Ltd., part of Springer Nature 2025
When researchers introduced the Transformer architec‑
ture in 2017, they titled their paper “Attention Is All You 
Need” (Vaswani et al. 2017). That phrase, originally meant 
to describe a new way for machines to process language, 
has since become emblematic of our era. The technology 
behind it has reshaped how we interact with information. 
Machines can now focus on every word, every query, and 
every fragment of context with astonishing precision. But 
there is a catch. What these systems deliver is not attention 
in the human sense. It is the performance of attention: fluent, 
responsive, and profoundly irresponsible.
That distinction matters.
